title: White House cherry-picks 3 deflation data points while oil surges 44% — 2026-05-18
content:
White House spokesperson Kush Desai stated: "The American economy remains on a solid trajectory... March CPI showed cooling core inflation and prices of beef, dairy, eggs, and prescription drugs actually declining thanks to the President's policies." Trump predicted on May 12 that inflation would drop as soon as the Iran war ends. The public narrative: the war's economic damage is contained and temporary, with the administration crediting its own policies for cooling prices.

## What the Data Says

- Brent crude up 44% since the war began; now trading at ~$105/barrel
- US gasoline averaging $4.06/gallon (pre-war level was $2.98)
- CPI reached 3.3% — highest since May 2024; PCE projected at 4% by year-end (double the Fed's 2% target)
- Dallas Fed research: war could add 0.2 to 1.8 percentage points to Q4/Q4 headline inflation in 2026
- EY-Parthenon projects GDP drag of 0.3 percentage points, cutting growth to 1.8% vs. 2.1% in 2025
- IEA predicts global natural gas supplies will remain tight for two years due to the conflict
- Mark Zandi (Moody's): "The damage has already been done, in part because there's no going back on oil prices, at least not any time in the near future"
- Desai cited beef, dairy, eggs (deflating) while ignoring energy — the exact sector driving the entire CPI surge
- Gas expected to settle at $3.50 at best by year-end — still above pre-war levels

## The Real Story

Headline: "The Three Price Drops the White House Cited vs. The One That Matters" — dissect the cherry-picked inflation narrative. The administration found deflation in three narrow food categories while Brent crude surged 44%, gas hit $4.06, and the Fed's preferred inflation gauge is tracking toward 4%. A case study in selective data deployment to manage a narrative around an externally-caused crisis.

Key quote: Zandi — "The damage has already been done."
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The White House Is Winning the Narrative War on Inflation by Cherry-Picking Three Data Points While Oil Surges 44%. Selective citation of cooling beef/dairy/egg prices ignores the energy input costs driving everything else.
